title: onopordum × humile var. turolense
intro: Onopordum × humile var. turolense, also known as False Cotton Thistle or Cardo Mariano, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Onopordum, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone compounds. The health benefits of Onopordum × humile var. turolense include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be applied topically in the form of a poultice or compress.
